fresh_facts.claim: US warplanes took off toward Iran on March 10, 2026 as part of ongoing operations targeting Iranian military infrastructure including missile launchers.
President Trump stated on March 9-10, 2026 that Iran's navy, air force, communications, missiles, and drones are nearly destroyed.
Israeli Air Force conducted strikes on six IRGC air bases overnight into March 10, 2026, destroying aircraft, helicopters, runways, and air defenses.
Over 170 bombs were dropped on Tehran, East Fahan, and Shiraz by Israeli forces on March 9-10, 2026.
Iran's new Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei was wounded, according to Iranian state TV on March 9, 2026.
Mojtaba Khamenei was appointed as Iran's next supreme leader by the Assembly of Experts according to reports from March 8, 2026.
Iran launched attacks on Israel and Gulf states on March 9-10, 2026, including missiles and drones that were intercepted by Kuwaiti forces.
An official told Fars News on March 9, 2026 that Iran will intensify offensives against Israel and the US from March 9 night onward.
Iran reported 104 crew members killed in a prior US attack on the IRIS Dena vessel.
Israel killed Hezbollah's Nasr Unit head in south Lebanon overnight March 9-10, 2026.
NATO intercepted a Turkey-bound Iranian ballistic missile on March 9, 2026.
Iran's UN ambassador reported over 1,330 civilian deaths, including 300 children, from US-Israeli strikes as of March 9, 2026.
Hardline Iranian officials expressed dissatisfaction with President Masoud Pezeshkian's statement about Iranian attacks on regional nations on March 7, 2026.

President Pezeshkian initially expressed regret to affected nations and stated the Leadership Council instructed the Armed Forces to refrain from attacking unless provoked, but shifted to a harder line by March 8, 2026.
Iranian authorities reported attacks on four oil storage tankers and a petroleum transfer terminal around Tehran on March 8, 2026, resulting in four deaths.
The Iranian Foreign Ministry characterized recent strikes as 'intentional chemical warfare' and warned of toxic air and acid rain risks on March 8, 2026.
Saudi Arabia warned Tehran on March 8, 2026 that further attacks on its territory or energy sites could trigger retaliation and potentially open Saudi bases to US operations.
Coalition airstrikes targeted the Parchin Military Complex, Shahroud Missile Facility, and Khojir Missile Production Complex in Iran.
President Trump stated on March 5, 2026 that he wanted involvement in choosing Khamenei's successor and considered Mojtaba Khamenei 'unacceptable' as Iran's leader.
Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth noted on March 10, 2026 that military strikes avoid 'endless nation-building.'
Prime Minister Netanyahu stated on March 10, 2026 that joint US-Israeli operations are 'breaking Iran's bones' and far from over.
IDF struck Beirut targets including rocket launchers overnight March 9-10, 2026.
Iranian authorities warned of potential oil facility strikes in retaliation as of March 9, 2026.
According to ACLED analysis, despite the unprecedented military campaign, full Iranian capitulation remains unlikely and hardline IRGC elements are likely dominating decision-making.
The US-Israeli war with Iran began on February 28, 2026.

regime_fall.current_status: Ongoing intensive US-Israeli military campaign continues with hundreds of additional strikes on Iranian military infrastructure, air bases, and missile facilities as of March 10, 2026. New Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei has been wounded, while Iran attempts continued military responses despite severe degradation of capabilities.
regime_fall.last_major_event: US warplanes and Israeli Air Force conducted additional major strikes on March 10, 2026, targeting IRGC air bases, missile facilities, and military infrastructure, with Israeli forces dropping over 170 bombs across Tehran, East Fahan, and Shiraz.
